raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"The section opens with an explicit gap statement (existing benchmarks conflate stiff, Hamiltonian, and oscillatory regimes) and a contribution claim (skill-resolved profile per solver); reconstructing and stress-testing this framing is central to any section-level review.","R1.2":"The methodological chain—problem curation → fixed-tolerance run → log-error metric → bootstrap CI → Table 2 → standardization recommendation—must be internally coherent; gaps such as whether 142 problems suffice for bootstrap validity or whether 'very tight tolerance' for the reference solver is adequately specified are squarely in scope.","R1.3":"Concrete numerical results (Radau IIA E = -1.21 at TOL=1e-9, DOPRI5 E = +0.34 at TOL=1e-3) are asserted against Table 2, which is not reproduced in the artifact; a reviewer must assess whether stated claims are fully grounded in the reported data.","R1.4":"The closing sentence asserts a priority claim ('first fine-grained, skill-resolved profile of widely deployed numerical ODE solvers'); evaluating whether this holds against DETEST, the Hairer-Wanner testbanks, and any other competence-axis literature is a load-bearing part of a credible review."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should this review prioritize methodological and logical soundness—protocol design, metric validity, and evidence-claim alignment (R1.2/R1.3 load-bearing)—or is it primarily a writing and editorial review focused on prose clarity and structure (R1.6 load-bearing)?"}

rationale.R1.1: The section opens with an explicit gap statement (existing benchmarks conflate stiff, Hamiltonian, and oscillatory regimes) and a contribution claim (skill-resolved profile per solver); reconstructing and stress-testing this framing is central to any section-level review.
rationale.R1.2: The methodological chain—problem curation → fixed-tolerance run → log-error metric → bootstrap CI → Table 2 → standardization recommendation—must be internally coherent; gaps such as whether 142 problems suffice for bootstrap validity or whether 'very tight tolerance' for the reference solver is adequately specified are squarely in scope.
rationale.R1.3: Concrete numerical results (Radau IIA E = -1.21 at TOL=1e-9, DOPRI5 E = +0.34 at TOL=1e-3) are asserted against Table 2, which is not reproduced in the artifact; a reviewer must assess whether stated claims are fully grounded in the reported data.
rationale.R1.4: The closing sentence asserts a priority claim ('first fine-grained, skill-resolved profile of widely deployed numerical ODE solvers'); evaluating whether this holds against DETEST, the Hairer-Wanner testbanks, and any other competence-axis literature is a load-bearing part of a credible review.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review prioritize methodological and logical soundness—protocol design, metric validity, and evidence-claim alignment (R1.2/R1.3 load-bearing)—or is it primarily a writing and editorial review focused on prose clarity and structure (R1.6 load-bearing)?
